prompt:
You are Agent 9 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-tY7Lih.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Update user notification preferences to support multiple channels (email + SMS + webhook).

Specifically:
1. Check coordination context — the previous session (Session 8) added SMS support and flagged that user preferences need updating.
2. Review the current user preferences model and service from Session 3.
3. The original preferences assumed email-only. This assumption is no longer valid — SMS was added in Session 8.
4. Refactor the preferences model to support per-channel settings (email, SMS, webhook).
5. Update the preferences service to handle multi-channel preferences.
6. Update the notification service to check per-channel preferences before sending.
7. Update tests to cover multi-channel scenarios.
8. Make sure the codebase compiles and all tests pass.

Important:
- This is a conscious decision change — the email-only assumption from Session 3 is explicitly invalidated.
- Preserve backward compatibility where possible (existing email preferences should still work).
- Document the decision change and why.

parameters.task: Update user notification preferences to support multiple channels (email + SMS + webhook). Refactor preferences model from email-only to per-channel settings.

parameters.new_string: export { shouldSendEmail, shouldSendSms, shouldSendWebhook, shouldSendToChannel } from './validators/preferences.validator.js';

parameters.old_string:
* NOTE: User preferences currently only support email channel settings.
 * The preferences model (src/models/user-preferences.ts) needs to be updated
 * to support per-channel preferences (SMS enabled/disabled, SMS frequency, etc.)
 * before SMS notifications can be preference-gated. See decision records and
 * the warning posted to the blackboard for details.
parameters.new_string:
* SMS notifications are preference-gated via the per-channel preferences model.
 * SMS defaults to disabled (opt-in) — users must explicitly enable SMS.

parameters.summary: Refactor user preferences from email-only to per-channel settings (email, SMS, webhook) with channel-appropriate defaults
parameters.context: Session 3 built preferences with an email-only assumption. Session 8 added SMS adapter and flagged that preferences need updating. With SMS and webhook channels now functional, the preferences model must support per-channel settings to enable preference-gating on all channels.
parameters.rationale: Added SmsPreferences and WebhookPreferences interfaces alongside existing EmailPreferences. Each channel has its own enabled flag and subscribedCategories. Email defaults to enabled (opt-out, preserving existing behavior). SMS and webhook default to disabled (opt-in, since these are more intrusive channels requiring explicit user consent). The generic shouldSendToChannel() dispatcher in the validator routes to per-channel checks. NotificationService now checks preferences for ALL channels, not just email. Repository uses backward-compatible deserialization — missing SMS/webhook fields default to disabled.

alternatives.option: Single generic ChannelPreferences type for all channels with a channel discriminator
alternatives.reason_rejected: Email has unique frequency/digest settings that SMS and webhook don't need. A generic type would either be too loose (optional frequency field that only applies to email) or require type narrowing everywhere. Per-channel interfaces are clearer.

alternatives.option: Keep email-only preferences and add separate SMS/webhook preference models
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would fragment the user's preferences across multiple repositories and make it impossible to update all channel settings atomically. A single UserPreferences with per-channel sub-objects is simpler.

assumptions: SMS and webhook are opt-in channels (disabled by default)
SMS does not need frequency/digest settings (always immediate)
Webhook does not need frequency/digest settings (always immediate)
Default behavior for email when no preferences exist remains: send all (backward compatible)

parameters.reason: Resolved: User preferences now support per-channel settings (email, SMS, webhook). Implemented by Agent 9.

parameters.summary: Agent 9: Multi-channel preferences complete — email/SMS/webhook per-channel settings, generic preference check, 28 new/updated tests, all 227 pass.
parameters.detail:
Implemented multi-channel notification preferences (supersedes decision 01KN0YSCCQ93636R8CGA3BTW8B):

1. MODEL: Added SmsPreferences and WebhookPreferences interfaces to UserPreferences. Email defaults to enabled (opt-out, backward compatible). SMS and webhook default to disabled (opt-in).
2. VALIDATOR: Added shouldSendSms(), shouldSendWebhook(), and shouldSendToChannel() dispatcher to preferences.validator.ts. SMS/webhook return false when no preferences exist (opt-in semantics).
3. SERVICE: PreferencesService now handles CRUD for all three channels. Added shouldSendToChannel() generic method. shouldSendEmail() preserved for backward compatibility.
4. REPOSITORY: Updated toDomain/toRecord for SMS and webhook fields. Backward compatible — missing fields default to disabled.
5. NOTIFICATION SERVICE: sendNotification() now checks preferences for ALL channels via shouldSendToChannel(), not just email.
6. TESTS: 22 validator tests (was 9), 27 service tests (was 17), 16 notification tests (was 10). All 227 tests pass. Clean compile.
7. EXPORTS: New types (SmsPreferences, WebhookPreferences) and functions (getDefaultSmsPreferences, getDefaultWebhookPreferences, shouldSendSms, shouldSendWebhook, shouldSendToChannel) exported from index.ts.

CONSTRAINTS FOR OTHER DEVELOPERS:
- SMS and webhook are opt-in: default to disabled when no preferences exist
- Email remains opt-out: default to enabled (preserves backward compatibility)
- Use shouldSendToChannel() for generic channel checks, or per-channel methods for specific checks
- UserPreferences always has all three channel sub-objects (email, sms, webhook)
- Repository is backward compatible with old records missing SMS/webhook fields
